Defendant Scott Peterson'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Philip Milestone, Esq. of Milestone Legal P.C., 510.508.1626, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Gener D. Benitez, Esq. of Ibarra Professional Law Corporation, 415.398.5329, appearing for Defendant.

No argument held.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Defendant Scott Peterson's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is off calendar in light of the ruling on his motion to compel arbitration. Presumably Mr. Peterson will assert the claims in his proposed cross-complaint as counterclaims in the arbitration proceedings. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com/mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/HEK)

Defendant Scott Peterson'S Motion To (1) Compel Arbitration And (2) Stay Of Action Until The Completion Of Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Philip Milestone, Esq. of Milestone Legal P.C., 510.508.1626, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Gener D. Benitez, Esq. of Ibarra Professional Law Corporation, 415.398.5329, appearing for Defendant.

Ruling: ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Scott Peterson's motion to compel arbitration and to stay action until the completion of arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending completion of arbitration proceedings on all claims alleged by plaintiffs Theodore Wilson and Tradingpost Modern LLC. The 2014 Operating Agreement includes an arbitration clause with a broad scope that also contains clear and unmistakable language evidencing the parties' intent to delegate issues of arbitrability to the arbitrator. This delegation language is not unconscionable and is enforceable. Plaintiffs have not shown that Mr. Peterson waived his right to enforce the arbitration clause. (St. Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1195 ("waivers are not to be lightly inferred and the party seeking to establish a waiver bears a heavy burden of proof.")). Mr. Peterson's actions are not inconsistent with intent to arbitrate. There is no evidence that "the litigation machinery has been substantially invoked." Nor is there any evidence of bad faith delaying tactics by Mr. Peterson. Most significantly plaintiffs have not shown how they have been prejudiced by any litigation conduct by Mr. Peterson. (Groom v. Health Net (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1189, 1197 ("Waiver does not occur by mere participation in litigation. Prejudice in the context of waiver of the right to compel arbitration normally means some impairment of the other party's ability to participate in arbitration.")). Bodine v. United Aircraft Corp. (1975) 52 Cal.App.3d 940, 943-944 is distinguishable because there, defendant "[w]ithout ever asserting its right to arbitrate, [] participated in extensive litigation through the pleading, discovery, trial, new trial and appeal stages" culminating in a 10 year delay to demand arbitration. Since Mr. Peterson disputes that he has dissociated from the company because he has not been paid the value of his membership interest, the validity of the Amended Operating Agreement and all related issues such as the arbitrability of parties' disputes whether Mr. Wilson dissociated from Tradingpost Modern and whether Mr. Wilson had the authority to amend the original operating agreement must be decided by the arbitrator. Whether the parties entered into a valid settlement agreement is also to be decided by the arbitrator. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com/mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/HEK)
